---
title: "Aim4 _Ver_1"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-20"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Load Library
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(phyloseq)
```


```{r Load datasets}
# Load in the filtered and wrangled Parkinson's metadata
parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat = read.csv("../../Datasets/Metadata/parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat.csv", row.names = 1)

# Load in feature table exported from QIIME2
counts = read.delim('../../QIIME2_outputs/feature-table.txt', skip = 1, row.names = 1)

# Load in taxonomy table exported from QIIME2
taxonomy = read.delim('../../QIIME2_outputs/taxonomy.tsv', row.names = 1)

# Counts
counts_formatted = counts %>% as.matrix()

# Taxonomy
taxonomy_formatted = taxonomy %>% 
  separate(col = Taxon, 
           into = c("Domain","Phylum","Class","Order",
                    "Family","Genus","Species"),
           sep = ";", fill = "right") %>% 
  select(-Confidence) %>% 
  as.matrix()

# Create the phyloseq object
counts_formatted = counts %>% as.matrix()
ps = phyloseq(sample_data(parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat),
              otu_table(counts_formatted, taxa_are_rows = T),
              tax_table(taxonomy_formatted))

```


```{r Load the indicator taxa}
# Extract the indicator taxa obtained from aim2 
taxa_id <- c("4c8288bfbd76958c0c094d87b97650f8") #Escherichia-Shigella

# Convert phyloseq to relative abundance
ps_relab <- transform_sample_counts(ps, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Get the relative abundance of Escherichia-Shigella and wrangle to keep only Escherichia-Shigella

# Extract the phyloseq abundance
otu <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_relab))

# make samples = rows
if (taxa_are_rows(ps_relab)) {
  otu <- t(otu)
}

otu <- as.data.frame(otu)   

# keep only Escherichia-Shigella
sub <- otu[, taxa_id, drop = FALSE]

sub <- data.frame(
  SampleID = rownames(otu),
  rel_abund = otu[, taxa_id]
)

#Log transform it, since the not transformed data is not normalized 
sub$log_rel_abund <- log10(sub$rel_abund + 1e-6)

```


```{r Add to Metadata}
meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps_relab))
meta$SampleID <- rownames(meta)

meta2 <- merge(meta, sub, by="SampleID")
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
```{r Predict the MoCA based on the indicator taxa}
model <- lm(moca_total ~ log_rel_abund + Age + Sex + entacapone, data = meta2)

summary(model)
```
Age and E.shigella (0.0230) seems to be the  predictors for MoCA score. 
```{r Generate Plot for Aim4}

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(meta2, aes(x = log_rel_abund, y = moca_total)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  labs(
    title = "Association between Escherichia-Shigella relative abundance in PD patients and MoCA",
    x = "log10(Relative abundance + 1e-6) of Escherichia-Shigella",
    y = "MoCA score"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 8)

```
